segments.text: Your iPhone Air looks normal from the front, but flip it over and the entire computer is contained into this tiny camera bump. So basically everything,
the processor, antenna, modem, and even the camera. They squeezed everything onto that tiny plateau. But why was it designed this way when most phones spread components everywhere?
Traditional phones scatter their parts throughout their body. Makes sense. More space, easier cooling. But Apple wanted something different. They wanted to
break a record. They aimed for the thinnest phone ever, just 5.6 mm thick.
But this thing created an impossible engineering problem. At 5.6 mm, even the physical SIM card could not fit anymore.
So, Apple had to kill the SIM card tray and entirely go eSIM. And that also on a worldwide level. Their solution pack everything into what they call the
camera plate too. But here's the genius part most people will miss. They created a sandwiched motherboard with components on both sides. And the A19 chip was actually fitting inside the camera bump.
The rest of the logic board has to share that ultra thin body with the battery.
But somehow it still delivers a all day battery life. So Apple turned their biggest constraint into their biggest innovation. The camera bump isn't the compromise. It's actually the entire
computer. To know more why behind design subscribe.
